V-M-035.checks: Backup and restore contract must preserve PostgreSQL data, runtime secrets, AWG state, and VPN device/client linkage.
V-M-035.commands: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-22.xml docs/modules/M-035.xml docs/recovery/BACKUP-PHASE-22.xml docs/recovery/DRY-RUN-PHASE-22.xml docs/verification/V-M-035.xml && mygrace lint; planned non-production pg_dump/pg_restore dry-run plus checksum and secret-redaction evidence

module-checks.check-1: Backup manifest names every required artifact without exposing secret values.
module-checks.check-2: Restore validation compares table counts, migration state, and selected integrity invariants.
module-checks.check-3: AWG profile, subnet settings, and existing client compatibility feed M-034 cutover gates.
module-checks.check-4: Checksums are generated before transfer and verified before restore validation passes.
module-checks.check-5: Non-production pg_dump/pg_restore dry-run evidence is captured before Phase-22 is trusted for incidents.

evidence-matrix.evidence-1: XML parse and mygrace lint pass for Phase-22, M-035, and V-M-035.
evidence-matrix.evidence-2: Manifest lists PostgreSQL dump, protected runtime env, AWG/VPN state, checksums, counts, migration state, and restore notes.
evidence-matrix.evidence-3: Backup and restore logs show artifact names and validation decisions but no secret values.
evidence-matrix.evidence-4: Non-production pg_dump/pg_restore proves table counts and invariants before live use.
evidence-matrix.evidence-5: Real incident recovery still requires live target validation and operator cutover approval.
evidence-matrix.evidence-6: Restore integrity gates compare schema/migration state, checksums, table counts, selected row invariants, and no-secret-log review.
evidence-matrix.evidence-7: Non-production pg_dump/pg_restore rehearsal is blocked until a trusted bundle and isolated restore target are available.

scenarios.scenario-1: Restored database contains matching users, subscriptions, payments, devices, VPN clients, and migration state.
scenarios.scenario-2: Backup bundle lacks .env/runtime secret material needed to boot replacement servers.
scenarios.scenario-3: Backup bundle lacks AWG profile or device-client state needed to preserve client connectivity.
scenarios.scenario-4: Checksum or critical table-count mismatch is ignored and cutover proceeds.
scenarios.scenario-5: Backup or restore logs expose secret values, tokens, passwords, private keys, or raw .env content.
scenarios.scenario-6: Existing client configs are invalidated without explicit operator-approved rotation.

autonomy-safety.safe-local: Agents may design manifest schemas, redaction checks, and non-production dry-run validation artifacts.
autonomy-safety.requires-operator: Agents must not move live secrets, run incident cutover, or rotate VPN/client material without explicit operator control.
